content: clean-jsdoc-theme-example A repo which shows how to setup clean-jsdoc-theme Requirements 1. node.js, (Download Node.js). Make sure you have node.js version 12 or later. Older versions of the node might work but are not guaranteed. 1. npm or yarn. Installation You need to install jsdoc: Then you need to install clean-jsdoc-theme: Setup At first, you have to create a config file. You can name it anything but for simplicity, keep it as jsdoc.json. In jsdoc.json you have to add some properties: Look at this repo jsdoc.json file as an example. For more jsdoc configuration visit https://jsdoc.app/about-configuring-jsdoc.html For clean-jsdoc-theme opts visit 1. https://github.com/ankitskvmdam/clean-jsdoc-theme options 1. https://github.com/ankitskvmdam/clean-jsdoc-theme cheat-sheet Generate docs 1. Add a generate script to package.json. For more information you can see the package.json file in this repo. After adding the script to package.json you have to run the following command: If everything works well then you will see a docs folder will be generated in which you will have all the docs. Contact Mail me at: hello@ankdev.me License Licensed under the MIT license.

title: DataProcessor

description: This class extends the {@link BaseEntity} to provide async processing and event-driven updates.
content: DataProcessor Advanced data processing suite. This class extends the BaseEntity to provide async processing and event-driven updates. - Author: Ankit Kumar \ - Requires: module:CoreSchema - Tutorials: processing-guide --- Constructor Parameters - id (string) — The processor ID. - options (Object, optional) — Configuration. - options.timeout (number, optional, default: 5000) — Timeout in ms. --- Instance Methods Promise. " / Processes a data stream asynchronously. & x20;Use the internal DataProcessor streamEngine instead. Parameters - data (Array.\ ) — Array of strings to process. Returns - Promise. — The total count of processed items. Fires - DataProcessor event:dataProcessed Example - Since: 1.2.0 - Version: 2.0.1 Generates a sequence of IDs. Yields - string — The next formatted ID in the sequence. string" / Returns - string Static Methods boolean" / Utility to check if a value is a valid ID. Parameters - val (\ ) — The value to test. Returns - boolean TODO: - Add regex validation for stricter ID checks. Enums Current processing state. Type number Events Event fired when data is successfully processed. Properties - count (number) — Total items processed. Type object
